title: Systematic Review and Network Meta-Analysis of Antithrombotic Therapy Strategies
author: Xiaoxuan Gong1*
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.iijcccn

steps.title: Search databases
steps.instruction: Search databases: PubMed, Embase and Cochrane Central Search key words: (“atrial fibrillation” or “AF”) and (“percutaneous coronary intervention” or “PCI” or “coronary stent implantation”) and (“aspirin” or “P2Y 12 antagonist” or “P2Y 12 inhibitor” or “clopidogrel” or “ticagrelor” or “warfarin” or “vitamin K antagonist” or “VKA” or “dabigatran” or “rivaroxaban” or “apixaban” or “edoxaban” or “novel oral anticoagulant” or “new oral anticoagulant” or “NOAC”) Two reviewers search key words independently from the earliest possible search date till March 2017.

steps.title: clinical trials with at least one compared group; (
steps.instruction: clinical trials with at least one compared group; (

steps.title: study population of coronary artery disease...
steps.instruction: study population of coronary artery disease patients with paroxysmal, persistent, or permanent AF undergoing PCI; (

steps.title: treatment with antiplatelet drugs (aspirin and...
steps.instruction: treatment with antiplatelet drugs (aspirin and P2Y12 inhibitors) and/or anticoagulants (warfarin and new oral anticoagulants such as dabigatran, rivaroxaban, apixaban and edoxaban) with a target international normalized ratio (INR) of 2.0-3.0 if warfarin is used to reflect present standard practice; (

steps.title: outcomes involving main adverse cardiac and...
steps.instruction: outcomes involving main adverse cardiac and cerebrovascular events (MACCEs) and major bleeding; (

steps.title: mean or median follow-up period no less than 12 months
steps.instruction: mean or median follow-up period no less than 12 months. Exclusion criteria: (

steps.title: articles published only in the form...
steps.instruction: articles published only in the form of abstracts or in non-English languages; (

steps.title: antithrombotic therapies change differently in post-PCI; (
steps.instruction: antithrombotic therapies change differently in post-PCI; (

steps.title: vague compared treatments such as VKA vs
steps.instruction: vague compared treatments such as VKA vs. non-VAK.

steps.title: article type
steps.instruction: article type: clinical trial designs, reviews, meta analyses, letters, case reports, comments, editorials, survey and investigation; (

steps.title: without at least a control group...
steps.instruction: without at least a control group or non-AF patients underwent PCI included; (

steps.title: without MACCEs or major bleeding endpoints; (
steps.instruction: without MACCEs or major bleeding endpoints; (

steps.title: mean/median follow-up periods less than 1 year
steps.instruction: mean/median follow-up periods less than 1 year.

steps.title: Collected data
steps.instruction: Collected data: study design, sample size (all and in compared arms), follow-up duration, antithrombotic strategies in post-PCI, efficacy and safety outcomes, baseline characteristics of the patients including mean age, male percentage, mean CHADS 2 /CHA 2 DS 2 -VASc and HAS-BLED scores, the number and percentage of MACCEs (including all-cause mortality, cardiac death, myocardial infarction, target lesion revascularization and stroke) as primary outcome and major bleeding outcomes (defined by TIMI or the Bleeding Academic Research Consortium [BARC] criteria) as secondary outcome both in around 1-year follow-up in every arm.

steps.title: Assess the qualities of observational studies...
steps.instruction: Assess the qualities of observational studies by the Newcastle-Ottawa Scale (NOS, evaluation of selection, comparability and outcome) and randomized controlled trials (RCTs) by Jadad Scale (judgment of randomization, double blind, withdrawals and dropouts) respectively.

steps.title: Treat results for endpoint events as dichotomous data
steps.instruction: Treat results for endpoint events as dichotomous data. Use risk ratio (RR) and 95% confidence interval (CI) to estimate pooled results from studies. Conduct a Bayesian network meta-analysis in Addis 1.16.8 software by Markov chain Monte Carlo methods, which provides direct and indirect evidence for any given treatments in one joint analysis and a possible ranking distribution of 2 endpoints for all regimens . If 95% CI of inconsistency factors median contains 0 and p value of node split model is over 0.05, inconsistent model will be considered not significant. Then use consistent model for further analysis.
